source: independent (non-author) pred-scout-coach-critic reviewing data/postgame/*.json coaching against the match facts; preference-based / ungrounded lines flagged; grounded rewrites applied back

3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.quote: Murdock carry, 7/5/2 — best damage on the team and the matchup into Sparrow is a coin-flip (48.4%), so this was yours to carry and you nearly did. Two fixes: 5 deaths vs your 3 norm means you got caught chasing out of position, and you skipped Lightning Hawk — the 53% core opener over 346 games. As the main threat on a 4/5-physical team that the enemy answers with a single armor piece, you need the crit/attack-speed spike from the core, not extra raw AD off Necrosis/Spear.
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.issue: source labels Murdock vs Sparrow favored, not a coin-flip
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.rewrite: Murdock carry, 7/5/2 — best damage on the team and the matchup into Sparrow read favored (48.4%), so this was yours to carry and you nearly did. Two fixes: 5 deaths vs your 3 norm means you got caught chasing out of position, and you skipped Lightning Hawk — the 53% core opener over 346 games. As the main threat on a 4/5-physical team that the enemy answers with a single armor piece, you need the crit/attack-speed spike from the core, not extra raw AD off Necrosis/Spear.

3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.quote: Grux jungle, 9/9/5 — and don't let the kills flatter it. You have 343 games on Grux at a 53.9% winrate and the matchup is actually favored (51.5% into Yin over 50k games), so 9 deaths — triple your own 3-death norm — isn't the draft, it's you. You stacked six damage items (Manta-Scythe, Terminus, Tainted-Trident, Deathstalker, Vanquisher) and skipped both Basilisk AND every point of magic resist into a 3-magical comp with Fey sitting on 81k. A 343-game one-trick should know not to dive a Fey lineup as a wet paper bag. Every one of those 9 deaths handed back the prime control the rest of the team kept winning. Build Basilisk, buy one magic resist, and stop feeding the magic burst — the favored jungle going even is how this loss happens.
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.issue: grades the pick by player's own game-count/winrate rather than the matchup
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.rewrite: Grux jungle, 9/9/5 — don't let the kills flatter it. The matchup read favored (51.5% into Yin), so 9 deaths — triple the 3-death norm — was the play, not the draft. You stacked six damage items (Manta-Scythe, Terminus, Tainted-Trident, Deathstalker, Vanquisher) and skipped both Basilisk and every point of magic resist into a 3-magical comp with Fey on 81k. Every one of those deaths handed back the prime control the rest of the team kept winning. Build Basilisk, buy one magic resist, and stop feeding the magic burst.

3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.quote: TwinBlast mid, 2/6/7 — the lowest-impact line on your side. You have 15 games on the hero at 53.3% and a dead-even matchup into Fey (48.5%), so 6 deaths at triple your 2-death norm with only 2 kills is just getting rolled in a lane you had no excuse to lose. The build made it worse: you ran TWO anti-heal items (Mindrazor and Vanquisher) against a comp whose only healer is Riktor — a support, not a heal-stack — while skipping Ashbringer and Imperator, two-thirds of TwinBlast's own 52.7%-winning core. Anti-heal isn't your job when you're 2/6; surviving and hitting your power spike is. Build the core, drop the double anti-heal, stop dying for free.
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.issue: grades the pick by player's own game-count/winrate rather than the matchup
3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.rewrite: TwinBlast mid, 2/6/7 — the lowest-impact line on your side. The Fey matchup read unfavored (48.5%), but 6 deaths at triple the 2-death norm with only 2 kills is getting rolled in a lane that was close on paper. The build made it worse: you ran TWO anti-heal items (Mindrazor and Vanquisher) against a comp whose only healer is Riktor — a support, not a heal-stack — while skipping Ashbringer and Imperator, two-thirds of TwinBlast's winning core. Anti-heal isn't the priority when you're 2/6; surviving and hitting your power spike is. Build the core, drop the double anti-heal, stop dying for free.
